### plan
Objective: Add a `verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>` function plus `LockVerifyStatus` enum and `LockVerifyEntry` struct to `core/src/registry.rs`, enabling grove doctor (T07) to recompute each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compare it against the lock file.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New `pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing }` in registry.rs; New `pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual: Option<String>, status }` in registry.rs; New `pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>` — Ok(None) when lock absent, Ok(Some(vec)) when parsed; Wasm resolved via `registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm` (same path as `resolve()`; covers dev-stubs, cache_root, GROVE_REGISTRY); NotFound error on wasm path → Missing; hash mismatch → Mismatch; equal hashes → Match; Reuses existing `sha256()` primitive so a lock written by `write_lock_for` verifies clean; Four unit tests: absent-lock (Ok(None)), Match after write_lock_for, Mismatch on tampered hash, Missing on nonexistent lang wasm; Add `pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus}` to core/src/lib.rs curated surface; No new files, no Cargo.toml changes, no lock-format changes — purely additive; Not material: additive read-only primitive, no CLI surface, no version bump required
Full artifact: PLAN.md
### review_plan
Objective: Assess the plan to add a read-only grove.lock wasm sha256 verify primitive (verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry) to core/src/registry.rs for grove doctor (T07).
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Verified against actual code: lock JSON shape {version,grammars:[{name,version,wasm}]} matches write_lock_for@412; plan's parse of doc[grammars]/entry[wasm]/name is correct.; AC#2 holds: wasm_sha256@106 == sha256(self.wasm) and verify_lock recomputes sha256 over the same file bytes, so a fresh lock verifies all-Match.; Wasm path registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm equals resolve@219's idx.root.join(lang)/grammar.wasm in steady state (index root seeded from registry_root).; Missing/Mismatch/None signals map correctly to NotFound / hash-diff / absent-file; Ok(None) is a clean non-error signal (AC#3).; Change is purely additive (new public types+fn, no signature/lock-format change) — matches 'no version bump' operational impact.; Advisory: index() memoizes root via OnceLock while verify_lock re-evaluates registry_root(); keep the Match test registry-root-agnostic (no mid-process GROVE_REGISTRY mutation) to avoid divergence.; Advisory: decide malformed-entry handling (entry missing wasm/name) deliberately; avoid silent unwrap.; Advisory: verify_lock takes the lock file path (like locked_langs), not a project root — T07 must pass the resolved grove.lock path.; All five acceptance criteria addressed; no changes required before implementation.
Findings: See key_changes — all findings mirrored there for schema compliance.
Full artifact: PLAN_REVIEW.md
### implementation
Objective: Add verify_lock(path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>> plus LockVerifyStatus enum and LockVerifyEntry struct to core/src/registry.rs, enabling grove doctor (T07) to recompute each cached grammar wasm's sha256 and compare it against the lock file.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: Added pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing } to registry.rs; Added pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual: Option<String>, status } to registry.rs; Added pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>> — Ok(None) when lock absent; Wasm resolved via registry_root()/<name>/grammar.wasm (same path as resolve()); NotFound I/O error → Missing; hash equality → Match; hash mismatch → Mismatch; Other I/O errors propagated via anyhow with context; Added verify_lock_returns_none_when_file_absent unit test; Added verify_lock_matches_after_write_lock_for unit test (fresh lock verifies all Match); Added verify_lock_detects_tampered_hash unit test (mutated wasm field → Mismatch); Added verify_lock_detects_missing_wasm unit test (nonexistent lang → Missing); Added pub use registry::{LockVerifyEntry, LockVerifyStatus} to core/src/lib.rs curated surface; All 165 unit tests pass; 4 new verify_lock tests all green
Full artifact: PROGRESS.md
### code_review
Objective: Reviewed the additive verify_lock primitive plus LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry types in core/src/registry.rs and the lib.rs re-export against the approved plan.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: All 8 plan/AC items met: types, verify_lock signature, wasm path resolution, status mapping, hash-format consistency, curated re-export, purely additive.; Independently ran cargo test verify_lock: 4 passed, 0 failed (161 filtered = 165 total, matches claim).; cargo clippy -p grove-cst --release --locked: clean, zero warnings.; Ok(None) on absent lock; NotFound->Missing; hash-eq->Match; diff->Mismatch; other I/O errors propagated with anyhow context (no silent swallow).; Fresh lock verifies all-Match: verify_lock reuses sha256() and write_lock_for pins wasm_sha256() delegating to same primitive.; Malformed entries (missing name/wasm) are continue-skipped, not unwrapped — deliberate non-panicking choice per plan-review advisory.; Doc comment correctly directs T07 to pass the resolved grove.lock file path, not a project root.; Tests authentic and meaningful (round-trip Match, tampered->Mismatch, nonexistent->Missing, absent->None).; Advisory (non-blocking): lock top-level version field not validated; fine for read-only advisory primitive.; Advisory (non-blocking): temp-test files share process id but use distinct name stems; no collision.
Findings: All 8 plan/AC items met and independently verified.; cargo test verify_lock: 4/4 pass; clippy clean.; Correct Match/Mismatch/Missing/None signal mapping; errors propagated not swallowed.; Additive-only: no signature, lock-format, or Cargo.toml changes.
Full artifact: CODE_REVIEW.md
### validation
Objective: Validated that verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry are correctly implemented in core/src/registry.rs against all five acceptance criteria.

No regressions.

# Commit Task
## Iron Laws

- Commit only the artifacts produced for this task; do not sweep unrelated working-tree changes into the commit. The commit boundary mirrors the task boundary.
- Read `.forge/personas/engineer.md` first; print the persona identity line (emoji, name, tagline) to stdout before any other tool use.
- All store I/O via `forge_store` (or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs`). Never edit `.forge/store/*.json` directly.
- **Never run `git add`/`git commit`/`git reset` yourself** — `commit-task.cjs` owns staging, boundary checks, committing, and the terminal transition (#40). Your judgement input is the message.
- **Commit writes NO summary** (`commit` ∉ `VALID_SUMMARY_PHASES` — any `set-summary` is rejected); the tool's terminal `update-status` is this phase's only store write.

## Store-Write Verification

<!-- See _fragments/store-write-verification.md for the canonical block content -->

## Algorithm

```

0. Entity-mode resolution:
   - Read the kickoff arguments. `--task {id}` → `entity_kind = "task"`, `record_id = {id}`. `--bug {id}` → `entity_kind = "bug"`, `record_id = {id}`.

1. Inspect ONCE (message material only — #40 batched-inspection rule):
   - One `git diff --stat`; at most ONE combined `git diff` if the message needs detail.
     Never per-file diffs, never repeated `git status` — each extra turn re-pays full context.
   - Staging is NOT your decision — the tool derives it from the store record.

2. Craft the commit message:
   - Follow project conventions; include the record ID ({taskId} / {bugId}) in the subject.
   - `Co-authored-by:` trailer from the host runtime: Claude Code →
     `Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>`; pi / Ollama / other →
     `Co-authored-by: {modelId} <noreply@{provider}.ai>` from the session's `provider` and
     `modelId`; if unresolvable, omit rather than guess. Never hardcode
     `Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>` (forge#82 regression).
   - Git's configured `user.name`/`user.email` own authorship; never `--author`.

3. Commit via the tool — ONE call:
   - If the `forge_commit` named tool is available (forgecli): call it —
     `forge_commit({ entity:"{entity_kind}", id:"{record_id}", message:"<message>", trailer:"<line>" })`.
     Never pass the message through a bash string when the typed tool exists.
   - Otherwise (Claude Code): `node .forge/tools/commit-task.cjs --{entity_kind} {record_id} --message "<message>" [--trailer "<Co-authored-by line>"]`
   - The tool owns the choreography: preflight gate (`preflight-gate.cjs --phase commit`
     internally), status precondition (task `approved` / bug `in-progress` — wrong-state runs
     halt with `× {record_id} is in state '{status}' …; /forge:approve must complete first`),
     staging (artifact dir + `summaries.implementation.files_changed` provenance),
     commit-boundary guard (aborts on a pre-staged index), `git commit`, terminal transition
     (task → `committed`; bug → `fixed`, the ONLY post-triage `bug.status` write).
   - On `no files_changed provenance` warning: ONE `git status --porcelain`, then re-run the
     tool with `--also <path>` per source file. Never manual `git add`.
   - Success → JSON with `ok:true`. `committed:true` carries `sha` + `staged`;
     `committed:false, reason:"nothing-to-commit"` is ALSO success (fix already at HEAD /
     staging set clean — the tool still sealed the record's terminal status). Do not
     "fix" a no-op by staging things yourself.
   - Failure (exit 1 / ok:false) → print stderr and HALT — no manual staging, no
     `git reset`, no `--force` retry (operator-gated). Tool file missing → HALT;
     instruct `/forge:update` + `/forge:rebuild tools`.
   - NEVER commit before the tool reports `ok: true` — the premature-commit/reset/redo loop
     is forbidden.

4. Finalize:
   - No summary, no `set-summary` (see Iron Laws). **Do NOT emit a phase event yourself** —
     the orchestrator owns event emission. Return the tool's JSON result as your phase output.
```

<!-- See _fragments/generation-instructions.md for Generation Instructions template -->

content.thinking: Now I have enough material for the commit message. The task adds `verify_lock` + `LockVerifyStatus`/`LockVerifyEntry` to `core/src/registry.rs` and re-exports them from `core/src/lib.rs`. Let me commit via forge_commit.

content.text: The key artifacts for GROVE-S03-T06 are `core/src/registry.rs` and `core/src/lib.rs`. Now I'll commit via the tool:

arguments.message:
feat(registry): add verify_lock + LockVerifyStatus/LockVerifyEntry (GROVE-S03-T06)

Add a read-only grove.lock verification primitive to core/src/registry.rs:

- `pub enum LockVerifyStatus { Match, Mismatch, Missing }`
- `pub struct LockVerifyEntry { lang, expected, actual, status }`
- `pub fn verify_lock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<Vec<LockVerifyEntry>>>`
  - Ok(None) when lock file is absent
  - Reuses sha256() to recompute each cached grammar wasm's hash
  - NotFound I/O error → Missing; hash diff → Mismatch; equal → Match
  - Other I/O errors propagated via anyhow with context
  - Malformed entries (missing name/wasm) are skipped (non-panicking)
- Re-export `LockVerifyEntry`, `LockVerifyStatus` via core/src/lib.rs curated surface
- 4 unit tests: absent-lock (Ok(None)), Match (round-trip), Mismatch (tampered), Missing

Purely additive; no signature, lock-format, or Cargo.toml changes. Enables grove doctor (T07).

content.text:
Commit succeeded. ✅

**SHA:** `90c1f2d3414c05ddc27af888b690bad99470d778`

**Staged (16 files):**
- `core/src/registry.rs` — new `LockVerifyStatus`, `LockVerifyEntry`, `verify_lock` + 4 unit tests
- `core/src/lib.rs` — curated re-export of the two new types
- All GROVE-S03-T06 phase artifacts (PLAN.md, PLAN_REVIEW.md, PROGRESS.md, CODE_REVIEW.md, VALIDATION_REPORT.md, and their JSON summaries)

Task `GROVE-S03-T06` is now **committed**. The `verify_lock` primitive is sealed and ready for T07 (grove doctor) to consume.
